div.p: Anatomy of a representative QNet (HIR) op, e.g. qnet.rot_x. Show:

ul.li: Operands on the left: input qubit value (!qnet.qubit), angle (f32).
The op box in the middle: qnet.rot_x with its traits annotated (QubitOpIface, RotationOpIface).
Result on the right: a fresh qubit value (!qnet.qubit).

div.p: Use this figure to convey the central HIR property: ops are value-to-value — they consume the input qubit value and produce a new one, with no implicit memory side effects. Optionally include a small "before / after" SSA chain to make the linearity invariant visible.
